info.title: BetDEX Wallet Integration API
info.description: A RESTful API that allows you to interact with the BetDEX Exchange. Provides endpoints for managing Events, Markets, Sessions, Wallets and Orders. To interact with the API, a client must have an App Id and an API Key to authenticate and create a session.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of markets based on the provided filter and ordering criteria. This can get specific known markets (by market ID), or markets based on other criteria such as event ID(s) and/or market type and status etc.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Optional list of specific IDs to fetch

parameters.name: ownerAppIds
parameters.description: Optional list of app IDs to filter results by

parameters.name: eventIds
parameters.description: Optional list of event IDs to filter results by

parameters.name: marketTypeIds
parameters.description: Optional list of market type IDs to filter results by

parameters.name: currencyIds
parameters.description: Optional list of currency IDs to filter results by

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Optional list of market statuses to filter results by

parameters.name: inPlayStatuses
parameters.description: Whether the markets are currently in-play or not. Returns all if not set

parameters.name: fromDateTime
parameters.description: The inclusive beginning of a date-time range filter. If not provided, no restriction will be applied to the beginning of the range

parameters.name: toDateTime
parameters.description: The exclusive end of a date-time range filter. If not provided, no restriction will be applied to the end of the range

parameters.name: published
parameters.description: Optional filter as to whether the market is published or not

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Zero-based page index (0..N)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The size of the page to be returned

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property,(asc|desc). Default sort order is ascending. Multiple sort criteria are supported.

get.description: Fetch historical (settled) markets, filtered by query type (settlement date or event start date). Optionally filter by event. Pagination is by offset using the selected queryType timestamp (settledAt or eventExpectedStartTime) and id as the primary and secondary offsets. Fetch the initial page without any offsets set, and use returned _next_offset values iteratively until _next_offset is null, or _count is 0.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: beginning of the date range filter (inclusive)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: end of the date range filter (inclusive)

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: optional event id to filter results by

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Value to offset the current page request by (inclusive)

parameters.name: secondaryOffset
parameters.description: Secondary offset value, i.e., if offset value is non-unique (exclusive)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements to retrieve

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: asc|desc. "

Default sort order is ascending.

parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: market id

post.description: Submit a request to create an order and get back an acknowledgement response containing the order's ID immediately. An order with a status of Pending will be created immediately in order to generate an ID; Any in-play/BIR delay, as well as initial matching will be performed asynchronously. Updates about orders created via this method maybe found as OrderUpdate messages through the StreamAPI and/or by calling GET /orders/{id} with the returned ID

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

post.description: Submit a request to create multiple orders and get back an acknowledgement response containing the orders' ID's immediately. Orders with status of Pending will be created immediately in order to generate IDs; Any in-play/BIR delay, as well as initial matching will be performed asynchronously. The order IDs, and/or any errors, will be ordered in the response in the same order as in the request. Updates about orders created via this method maybe found as OrderUpdate messages through the StreamAPI and/or by calling GET /orders/{id} with the returned IDs

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

get.description: Fetch orders by filters. At least one of the following filter parameters must be supplied: ids, appIds, eventIds, marketIds, walletIds, references. Page size limit 500.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: list of order ids to filter by

parameters.name: appIds
parameters.description: list of apps to filter by

parameters.name: eventIds
parameters.description: list of events to filter by

parameters.name: marketIds
parameters.description: list of markets to filter by

parameters.name: walletIds
parameters.description: list of wallets to filter by

parameters.name: references
parameters.description: list of references to filter by - requires appId to be provided also

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
statuses to filter by - valid values to choose are as follows:
  • Cancelled - Orders that have not matched and have been cancelled either on request or automatically
  • Matched - Orders that have been matched, either fully or partially, where the remaining unmatched stake has been cancelled, either on request or automatically
  • PartiallyMatched - Orders that have been partially matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Unmatched - Orders that have not yet been matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Won - Previously Matched orders that have been deemed to have Won at the time of market settlement
  • Lost - Previously Matched orders that have been deemed to have Lost at the time of market settlement
  • Voided - All orders belonging to markets that have been voided

items.description:
The status of an order can be one of the following
  • Cancelled - The order was not matched and has been cancelled either on request or automatically
  • Failed - A pending order was persisted but then failed to be fully created
  • Matched - The order has been matched, either fully, or partially where the remaining unmatched stake has been cancelled, either on request or automatically
  • PartiallyMatched - The order has been partially matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Pending - An order has been persisted but has not yet been processed
  • Unmatched - The order has not yet been matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Won - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Won
  • Lost - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Lost
  • Voided - The market has been voided so this order has been voided

parameters.name: fromStake
parameters.description: beginning of the stake range filter (inclusive)

parameters.name: toStake
parameters.description: end of the stake range filter (inclusive)

parameters.name: fromCreatedAt
parameters.description: beginning of the creation date range filter (inclusive)

parameters.name: toCreatedAt
parameters.description: end of the creation date range filter (inclusive)

parameters.name: matchBehavior
parameters.description:
Optional - How any unmatched portion of an order is treated:
  • RetainUnmatched - unmatched portion persists as an offer (default)
  • CancelUnmatched - unmatched portion is cancelled

parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property,(asc|desc). Default sort order is ascending. Multiple sort criteria are supported.

post.description: Cancel orders owned by the caller by event and/or market; skips invalid markets/orders. Pending orders will be updated with matchBehavior: CancelUnmatched, and any remaining unmatched stake after processing will be cancelled.

parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

parameters.name: walletIds
parameters.description: list of wallets to filter the orders to be cancelled

parameters.name: eventIds
parameters.description: list of event IDs to filter the orders to be cancelled

parameters.name: marketIds
parameters.description: list of market IDs to filter the orders to be cancelled

parameters.name: orderIds
parameters.description: list of order IDs to filter the orders to be cancelled

post.description: Cancel orders owned by the caller by event and/or market; skips invalid markets/orders. Pending orders will be updated with matchBehavior: CancelUnmatched, and any remaining unmatched stake after processing will be cancelled.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the accessToken for the current session

200.description: Returns an IDResponse object containing the IDs of any successfully cancelled orders.

get.summary: Fetch cancelled and failed orders by wallet
get.description: Fetch cancelled (unmatched/unfilled) and failed orders for the given wallet that were created in the given date range. Optionally filter by market. Pagination is by offset using createdAt and id, as the primary and secondary offsets. Fetch initial page without any offsets set, and use returned _next_offset values iteratively until _next_offset is null, or _count is 0.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Value to offset the current page request by (inclusive)

parameters.name: secondaryOffset
parameters.description: Secondary offset value, i.e., if offset value is non-unique (exclusive)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements to retrieve

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: asc|desc. "

Default sort order is ascending.

get.summary: Fetch historical won, lost or voided orders by wallet
get.description: Fetch historical (won, lost or voided) orders for the given wallet, filtered by query type (settlement date or event start date). Optionally filter by market. Pagination is by offset using the selected queryType timestamp (settledAt or eventExpectedStartTime) and id as the primary and secondary offsets. Fetch the initial page without any offsets set, and use returned _next_offset values iteratively until _next_offset is null, or _count is 0.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Value to offset the current page request by (inclusive)

parameters.name: secondaryOffset
parameters.description: Secondary offset value, i.e., if offset value is non-unique (exclusive)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements to retrieve

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: asc|desc. "

Default sort order is ascending.

post.description: Create a session for the supplied credentials and receive access and refresh tokens. These tokens will be used for Bearer authentication, and passed as part of the authorization header where required. NOTE: walletId should be omitted or set to null unless the API key being used is of type WALLET.

404.description: No matching credentials were found. If the provided API key is of type WALLET, please be sure the appropriate wallet ID is passed in the walletId field.

example.title: Not found
details: APIKey entity with ID of pa****34 not found for App with ID of ANAPPID

post.description:
Refresh an existing session using the refresh token. If a session's access token has expired but the refresh token is still valid, the existing session can be refreshed.
New access and refresh tokens will be returned.

The refresh token should be used in place of the access token in the authorization header for Bearer authentication.

This is a faster operation than creating a new session and refreshing should be preferred to creating new sessions where possible.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Header with the refreshToken for the current session

requestBody.description: NOTE: This request body is deprecated, plase pass the refresh token as part of the authorization header instead

transactionUniqueIdentifier.description: Required: a unique identifier for this transaction, such as a Solana transaction id

transactionDescription.description: Required: a description for this transaction, such as the reason for the update

marketLockAction.description: Action to take when market is locked - default is to cancel unmatched liquidity

eventStartAction.description: Action to take when market's event start time passes, i.e., transitioning a market from PrePlay to InPlay - default is to cancel unmatched liquidity (can be managed per order)

keepWhenInPlay.description: Optional - whether to keep unmatched portion of the order when its market enters InPlay (only relevant if the market has eventStartAction: CancelUnmatchedLiquidity) - default is false

matchBehavior.description:
Optional - How any unmatched portion of an order is treated:
  • RetainUnmatched - unmatched portion persists as an offer (default)
  • CancelUnmatched - unmatched portion is cancelled

commissionRateId.description:
Optional - to use a different commission rate for this order than your app's
default commission rate. If omitted or set to null, the app's default
commission rate will be used (if it has one).

walletId.description: If using an API key of type WALLET, please supply the ID of the wallet

EventParticipant.description:
This model is deprecated and will be removed in future versions. Please use the Participant model instead.

A participant in a given event.

id.description: Globally unique ID for this participant across all subcategories

code.description: Unique code for this participant across all events in a given subcategory

marketLockAction.description: Action to take when market locks - default is to cancel unmatched liquidity

eventStartAction.description: Action to take when market's event start time passes, i.e., transitioning a market from PrePlay to InPlay - default is to cancel unmatched liquidity (can be managed per order)

keepWhenInPlay.description: Whether to keep unmatched portion of the order when its market enters InPlay (only relevant if the market has eventStartAction: CancelUnmatchedLiquidity)

status.description:
The status of an order can be one of the following
  • Cancelled - The order was not matched and has been cancelled either on request or automatically
  • Failed - A pending order was persisted but then failed to be fully created
  • Matched - The order has been matched, either fully, or partially where the remaining unmatched stake has been cancelled, either on request or automatically
  • PartiallyMatched - The order has been partially matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Pending - An order has been persisted but has not yet been processed
  • Unmatched - The order has not yet been matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Won - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Won
  • Lost - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Lost
  • Voided - The market has been voided so this order has been voided

matchBehavior.description:
Optional - How any unmatched portion of an order is treated:
  • RetainUnmatched - unmatched portion persists as an offer (default)
  • CancelUnmatched - unmatched portion is cancelled

cancellationReason.description: Only present for Cancelled or Failed orders - why an order is Cancelled or Failed

errorCode.description:
Order operation error code describing type of an error. Possible values:
MARKET_NOT_FOUND,
INVALID_MARKET_STATUS,
SUSPENDED_MARKET,
MARKET_OUTCOME_NOT_FOUND,
COMMISSION_RATE_NOT_FOUND,
WALLET_NOT_FOUND,
WALLET_INSUFFICIENT_CREDIT,
WALLET_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND,
WALLET_ACCOUNT_INSUFFICIENT_CREDIT,
MARKET_POSITION_NOT_FOUND,
ORDER_ALREADY_EXISTS,
INTERRUPTED

status.description:
The status of an order can be one of the following
  • Cancelled - The order was not matched and has been cancelled either on request or automatically
  • Failed - A pending order was persisted but then failed to be fully created
  • Matched - The order has been matched, either fully, or partially where the remaining unmatched stake has been cancelled, either on request or automatically
  • PartiallyMatched - The order has been partially matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Pending - An order has been persisted but has not yet been processed
  • Unmatched - The order has not yet been matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Won - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Won
  • Lost - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Lost
  • Voided - The market has been voided so this order has been voided

The status of an order can be one of the following
  • Cancelled - The order was not matched and has been cancelled either on request or automatically
  • Failed - A pending order was persisted but then failed to be fully created
  • Matched - The order has been matched, either fully, or partially where the remaining unmatched stake has been cancelled, either on request or automatically
  • PartiallyMatched - The order has been partially matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Pending - An order has been persisted but has not yet been processed
  • Unmatched - The order has not yet been matched, and the remaining unmatched portion might still be matched
  • Won - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Won
  • Lost - The market has been settled and this previously Matched order has been deemed to have Lost
  • Voided - The market has been voided so this order has been voided

id.description: Globally unique ID for this participant across all subcategories

code.description: Unique code for this participant across all events in a given subcategory

id.description: Globally unique ID for this participant across all subcategories

code.description: Unique code for this participant across all events in a given subcategory

RefreshSessionRequest.description: Pass the refresh token in the authorization header instead of using this request body

ToggleActionRequest.description: request to toggle cross-matching feature for a given market

profitLoss.description:
Profit or Loss for the trade, calculated as follows:
'For' trades
  • Winning; profitLoss = stake * (price-1)
  • Losing; profitLoss = (stake * -1)
  • Voided; profitLoss = 0
'Against' trades
  • Winning; profitLoss = stake
  • Losing; profitLoss = stake * (price-1) * (-1)
  • Voided; profitLoss = 0

Profit or Loss for the trade, calculated as follows:
'For' trades
  • Winning trade; profitLoss = stake * (price-1)
  • Losing trade, profitLoss = (stake * -1)
  • Voided trade, profitLoss = 0
'Against' trades
  • Winning trade, profitLoss = stake
  • Losing trade, profitLoss = stake * (price-1) * (-1)
  • Voided trade, profitLoss = 0

commissionRate.description: A value between 0.00 and 1.0, where e.g., 0.01 is treated as 1%

UpdateDateRequest.description: request body containing a date field with the new value (cannot be null)

UpdateNameRequest.description: request body containing a name field with the new value (cannot be null or blank)

total.description: orders total risked ('stake' for backers and 'stake * (price - 1)' for layers

open.description: metrics for open orders: total equals to a unmatched stake for all orders, count equals to a number all 'Unmatched' and 'PartiallyMatched' orders

matched.description: metrics for matched orders: total equals to a matched stake for all 'Matched' orders, count equals to a number all 'Matched' orders

cancelled.description: metrics for cancelled orders: this is now deprecated and this field will be removed in a subsequent release

settled.description: metrics for settled orders: total equals to a matched stake for all 'Matched' orders, count equals to a number all 'Won' or 'Lost' orders

voided.description: metrics for voided orders: total equals to a voided stake for all 'Voided' orders, count equals to a number all 'Voided' orders
